$comment: Seed registry. Update this file every time a tool is added, changed, or retired. Tool Manager Agent reads/writes this.

tools.name: fact_check_tool
tools.description: Wraps web search/fetch with the channel's fixed source-priority ranking. Stage 2 live-tested 2026-07-20 (Tests/stage2_fact_check_tool_test.md): correctly ranked a DOJ press release over secondary outlets, and correctly flagged two deliberately-unsupported bait claims rather than fabricating sources for them.
tools.provider: Anthropic web_search tool or equivalent search API

tools.name: elevenlabs_voice_tool
tools.description: Provider-agnostic voiceover generation wrapper; default provider ElevenLabs Studio. generate_music() (Eleven Music API) is now the background-music FALLBACK only, superseded by Tools/royalty_free_music_tool.md as default since it costs per-generation credits. Real live-test of generate_music blocked by a missing music_generation permission on the current API key. Stage 4 (2026-07-21): TTS live-tested as a real n8n HTTP node with the standalone ELEVENLABS_API_KEY - 7 full chapters rendered in one run (Tests/stage4_full_pipeline_n8n_test.md). CONSTRAINT found: Creator plan allows max 5 concurrent requests, and n8n batchInterval only spaces request starts - use batchSize 1 + batchInterval 20000ms for chapter batches. FIXED same day: switched to the /with-timestamps endpoint (real per-character alignment) after captions built from a separate estimate drifted badly on a real render - see this file's 'Real-timestamp captioning' section and Workflows/process_pipeline_audio.py.
tools.input_schema_ref: text: string, voice_id: string, chapter: string | mood_prompt: string, duration_seconds: number

tools.name: image_gen_tool
tools.description: Provider-agnostic image generation wrapper. Default: fal.ai + Flux schnell (real API, near-free at test volume). Leonardo/Replicate as fallback. Midjourney excluded from automation (no official API). Stage 4 (2026-07-21): the same contract also confirmed working as a real n8n HTTP Request node (Tests/stage4_n8n_local_bootstrap.md), then batch-proven in the full pipeline trial - 70 scene images generated and saved to disk in one n8n run with batchSize 1 / 1s spacing, zero failures (Tests/stage4_full_pipeline_n8n_test.md).

tools.name: remotion_assembly_tool
tools.description: Programmatic video assembly: audio + images + timing + burned-in captions (fixed 2026 karaoke-style spec) + effects. Renders both the 16:9 main video and 9:16 Shorts selected by Agents/shorts_agent.md. Live-tested locally 2026-07-20 (Node.js installed via winget, Remotion scaffolded via create-video): real ~18s render using actual Banfield assets (0001.png, Jimmy-voice TTS, a bed track) succeeded and was visually confirmed correct (captions, karaoke highlight, Ken Burns pan, audio mix). Found the caption-timing 2s-minimum rule doesn't work for continuous main-video narration (fixed: natural word-count timing for main video, 2s floor stays for Shorts only) and that chunking should prefer clause boundaries over blind word-count splits (not yet implemented).
tools.input_schema_ref: scenes: object[], audio_track: string, background_music_track: string, subtitle_track: string

tools.name: youtube_publish_tool
tools.description: Wraps YouTube Data API v3 upload + metadata; publish call is human-gated.

tools.name: seo_export_tool
tools.description: Formats SEO Agent's structured output into Templates/SEO.md. Validated 2026-07-20 via real session use (Tests/stage2_seo_export_and_github_sync_test.md) — Cases/brendan-banfield-double-murder/SEO.md is a real product of exactly this transform.

tools.name: github_sync_tool
tools.description: Checks/updates tool_registry.json and commits studio changes to GitHub. Validated 2026-07-20 via real, repeated session use (Tests/stage2_seo_export_and_github_sync_test.md) — 5 real merged PRs this session with real commit SHAs; this environment has the real git/GitHub access the spec's own note said a plain chat session wouldn't.

tools.name: mugshot_fetch_tool
tools.description: Two-track real-photo sourcing. Track 1: person photos (mugshot/court exhibit) from official public-record sources only (now including outlet-hosted photos with explicit official-source captions), always eyes-redacted before use via OpenCV Haar-cascade detection. Track 2: non-person photos from any source including news articles, per the channel owner's 2026-07-19 risk decision. Track 1 live-tested end-to-end 2026-07-20 on the real Banfield mugshot (Cases/brendan-banfield-double-murder/PersonPhotos.md) — found, verified, downloaded, and automatically redacted successfully. Track 2 tried on the same article, found no valid candidate that run.
tools.input_schema_ref: case_name: string, subjects: object[], scene_targets: object[]
tools.output_schema_ref: person_images: object[], scene_images: object[], not_found: object[]
tools.provider: county sheriff / state DOC inmate lookup / court press releases / outlet-hosted-with-official-attribution (person track); any outlet (scene track)

tools.name: royalty_free_music_tool
tools.description: Confirmed 2026-07-19 (direct read of Pixabay's API docs): no free music source checked has a public API. Finalized approach: channel owner hand-picked 10 tracks from Pixabay Music, stored at ProductionStudio/Assets/audio/music_bed/bed_01.mp3..bed_10.mp3 (gitignored). get_bed_track() picks one and loops/trims it per render (main video or Short) — no live search needed. Stage 1 live-tested 2026-07-19: all 10 files verified as valid MP3s (mutagen), real durations read (99-504s), pick-and-loop math confirmed against the real Banfield case (main video + 2 Shorts).
tools.provider: Pixabay Music (10 hand-picked tracks, local); ElevenLabs Music (automated fallback, paid)
